texto: Long pants,’ she said rather too fiercely to Andy when he came out of his room wearing shorts. ‘Long pants.’ She herded him back towards his door. ‘These people are from head office. These people are from Melbourne.’ And she emphasised Melbourne as if that should have been all he needed to know.‘Melbourne people vomit if they see knees,’ I told him, and he said, ‘Why is that kind of information never part of the briefing?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nick Earls: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Long pants,’ she said rather too fiercely to Andy when he came out of his room wearing shorts. ‘L…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, banter, brothers pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e brothers.

frase_original: Long pants,’ she said rather too fiercely to Andy when he came out of his room wearing shorts. ‘Long pants.’ She herded him back towards his door. ‘These people are from head office. These people are from Melbourne.’ And she emphasised Melbourne as if that should have been all he needed to know.‘Melbourne people vomit if they see knees,’ I told him, and he said, ‘Why is that kind of information never part of the briefing?
